subject: Re: GSJ
body:
please talk to me about this,.I think you should make the offer to kevin d'mour,, then follow it up in writing 

----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Monday, June 11, 2007 2:47:37 PM
Subject: FW: GSJ


Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein


-----Original Message-----
From: Farkas, Andrew [mailto:afarkas@islecap.com] 
Sent: Monday, June 11, 2007 1:33 PM
To: dkiesq@aol.com; Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: GSJ

We ultimately decided that going in the record with a writing was not
the best thing for us to do. This was especially true given the ferocity
and extent of the opposition to the czm application aired at the
hearing. Instead JE and I thought it best for me to meet pesonally with
Austin Monsanto, head of the czm committee, and to convey our sentiments
personally. I intend to do that when I am next in st thomas.  

----- Original Message -----
From: DKIESQ@aol.com <DKIESQ@aol.com>
To: Farkas, Andrew
Sent: Mon Jun 11 11:40:02 2007
Subject: GSJ

Andrew,

Jeffrey asked that I forward the attached draft letter to you for your
review.  I am lead to understand that Jeffrey and you discussed sending
a letter like this to Kjaer from IGY.  Please feel free to use whatever
portions, if any, of this letter that you might find useful.  We would
appreciate your letting us know how you ultimately proceeded in this
matter.

Thank you.

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: dkiesq@aol.com

subject: Re: GSJ
body:
in fact every negoatiaiton is different.. ,, WIth the
limitless i wasn't sure I wanted the deal at all. so
if the price was not appropriate,,,no need to go
further,, here  i really want the property and the
last point will be price...

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> I sent the e-mail to Kevin D'Amour as we discussed.
>  
> 1. Kevin advised me that Kevin had asked Bill Karr
> to forward to me by  
> e-mail the metes and bounds description of the
> offered property. Kevin did  not 
> know why Bill Karr did not forward the description,
> but Kevin promised to  
> contact Bill again to get Bill to send it to me.
>  
> I left messages today for Bill Karr at Bill's office
> (Bill's  secretary told 
> me that Bill is out of the office through the end of
> the year)  and on Bill's 
> mobile phone.  I will continue to follow up with
> Bill.   When I do reach Bill 
> I will ask Bill if there actually is a metes and
> bounds  description and, if 
> so, to give me a copy.  I will also ask Bill when 
> Kevin actually told Bill to 
> forward the description to me. 
>  
> 2. Kevin also asked me to explain to your mom that
> Kevin is still working  on 
> getting a response to my requests for more modest
> immediate permit and  
> construction rights on GSJ and for seller to
> reconsider the requirement of  your 
> "active support" and accept in lieu thereof a
> negative covenant of no  direct or 
> indirect opposition.  Kevin also asked that I
> explain to your mom  that the 
> process takes a long time because Kevin has to
> consult with 7 different  people 
> in order to give me any response.  Kevin promised to
> follow up with  his 
> clients and to get back to me as soon as he can.
>  
> One other thing . . .
>  
> If you do not mind, may I request clarification
> about something?  As a  
> matter of strategy, don't we need to secure the
> essential business terms before  
> negotiating the finer business points and legal
> details?  Isn't it better  to 
> get Kjaer to commit to price and a description of
> land to be conveyed before  
> pressing him and D'Amour about the legal refinements
> I have already  requested?  
> I ask because I thought that you previously
> chastised me about  this when you 
> thought I was negotiating legal terms before
> finalizing the main  business 
> points in Limitless's agreement relating to Isle de 
> Sol. 
>  
> To save you unnecessary agitation, I like to be able
> to anticipate your  
> wishes based upon previous issues you raised with
> me.  Wasn't the issue of  
> "primary business terms first" one of those times
> when you specifically raised  an 
> issue with me so that there would be no repeat
> behavior?  Can we go over  this 
> when you get a chance?
>  
> Thanks. 
>  
> Darren
>  
>  
> 


__________________________________________________
Do You Yahoo!?
Tired of spam?  Yahoo! Mail has the best spam protection around 
http://mail.yahoo.com
body_html:
in fact every negoatiaiton is different.. ,, WIth the
limitless i wasn't sure I wanted the deal at all. so
if the price was not appropriate,,,no need to go
further,, here i really want the property and the
last point will be price...

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

I sent the e-mail to Kevin D'Amour as we discussed.

  1. Kevin advised me that Kevin had asked Bill Karr
    to forward to me by
    e-mail the metes and bounds description of the
    offered property. Kevin did not
    know why Bill Karr did not forward the description,
    but Kevin promised to
    contact Bill again to get Bill to send it to me.

I left messages today for Bill Karr at Bill's office
(Bill's secretary told
me that Bill is out of the office through the end of
the year) and on Bill's
mobile phone. I will continue to follow up with
Bill. When I do reach Bill
I will ask Bill if there actually is a metes and
bounds description and, if
so, to give me a copy. I will also ask Bill when
Kevin actually told Bill to
forward the description to me.

  1. Kevin also asked me to explain to your mom that
    Kevin is still working on
    getting a response to my requests for more modest
    immediate permit and
    construction rights on GSJ and for seller to
    reconsider the requirement of your
    "active support" and accept in lieu thereof a
    negative covenant of no direct or
    indirect opposition. Kevin also asked that I
    explain to your mom that the
    process takes a long time because Kevin has to
    consult with 7 different people
    in order to give me any response. Kevin promised to
    follow up with his
    clients and to get back to me as soon as he can.

One other thing . . .

If you do not mind, may I request clarification
about something? As a
matter of strategy, don't we need to secure the
essential business terms before
negotiating the finer business points and legal
details? Isn't it better to
get Kjaer to commit to price and a description of
land to be conveyed before
pressing him and D'Amour about the legal refinements
I have already requested?
I ask because I thought that you previously
chastised me about this when you
thought I was negotiating legal terms before
finalizing the main business
points in Limitless's agreement relating to Isle de
Sol.

To save you unnecessary agitation, I like to be able
to anticipate your
wishes based upon previous issues you raised with
me. Wasn't the issue of
"primary business terms first" one of those times
when you specifically raised an
issue with me so that there would be no repeat
behavior? Can we go over this
when you get a chance?

Thanks.

Darren

Do You Yahoo!?
Tired of spam? Yahoo! Mail has the best spam protection around
http://mail.yahoo.com
